subject: [PATCH v6 09/10] dax/kmem: add sysfs interface for atomic whole-device hotplug

reviews.author: Gregory Price (author)
reviews.summary:
Author acknowledged that there was an issue with resource release on total failure and added a fixup patch to make the sysfs interface consistent with the probe failure path.

Author acknowledged that the swapoff path needs to drop the per-vswap spinlock before calling try_to_unmap(), agreed to restructure in a future version.
reviews.raw_body:
... snip snip snip ...

Sashiko pointed out a false-positive, but adding a fixup patch
here that adds additional consistency.

On total failure - release all resources.  This makes the sysfs
interface consistent with the probe failure path.

Just attaching a fixup here, since technically it's not a bug,
could fold in separately

~Gregory

---

From: Gregory Price <gourry@gourry.net>
Date: Tue, 30 Jun 2026 14:49:06 -0700
Subject: [PATCH] fixup! dax/kmem: add sysfs interface for atomic whole-device
 hotplug

---
 drivers/dax/kmem.c | 5 ++++-
 1 file changed, 4 insertions(+), 1 deletion(-)

diff --git a/drivers/dax/kmem.c b/drivers/dax/kmem.c
index 19effe0da3dc..f597d8a99c1f 100644
--- a/drivers/dax/kmem.c
+++ b/drivers/dax/kmem.c
@@ -380,8 +380,11 @@ static ssize_t state_store(struct device *dev, struct device_attribute *attr,
 		return rc;
 
 	rc = dax_kmem_do_hotplug(dev_dax, data, online_type);
-	if (rc < 0)
+	if (rc < 0) {
+		/* Total failure, drop the reservations we took. */
+		dax_kmem_cleanup_resources(dev_dax, data);
 		return rc;
+	}
 
 	data->state = online_type;
 	return len;
-- 
2.53.0-Meta

2026-06-30.patch_summary: This patch adds a sysfs interface to enable atomic whole-device hotplug operations for DAX kmem devices. The new 'state' attribute allows devices to be transitioned between states such as unplugged, online, and offline_kernel or offline_movable in an atomic manner. This resolves a race condition issue where another entity could operate on memory blocks while the device is being removed, causing hot-unplug failures or deadlocks. The patch also updates the unbind process to prevent resource leaks and deadlock situations.

reviews.author: Hannes Reinecke
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that the patch introduces two interfaces for atomic whole-device hotplug, both generating uevents, which could lead to conflicting udev rules and system instability. He suggested disabling or making configurable the generation of uevents for the old interface.
reviews.raw_body:
Speaking of which ...
With this patch we now have _two_ interfaces to do the same thing.
And both will be generating uevents.
Which is far from ideal (one could easily envision _conflicting_
udev rules, one set doing an 'online' on the old interface,
and another set doing an 'offline' on the new interface...)
Is there a way to not sending uevents for the old interface
or to make it configurable?
The old interface had the nasty side effect of generating
_tons_ of uevents during booting, and on larger machines we
even had seen udev acting as a fork-bomb during booting,
taking down the entire machine (we had to restrict udev
to 512 threads max to avoid that from happening).
So if we could disable uevents for the old interface
things would be _so_ much easier ...

Cheers,

Hannes
-- 
Dr. Hannes Reinecke                  Kernel Storage Architect
hare@suse.de                                +49 911 74053 688
SUSE Software Solutions GmbH, Frankenstr. 146, 90461 Nrnberg
HRB 36809 (AG Nrnberg), GF: I. Totev, A. McDonald, W. Knoblich

reviews.author: Gregory Price (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged that the 'offline' state is not needed in the new sysfs interface and confirmed it's been removed, which resolves the issue.
reviews.raw_body:
We do not allow "offline" in the new interface.

That fixes the entire issue - the blocks are gone, nothing to poke.

~Gregory

2026-07-01.patch_summary: This patch adds a sysfs interface to enable atomic whole-device hotplug operations for DAX kmem devices. The new 'state' attribute allows devices to be transitioned between states such as unplugged, online, and offline_kernel or offline_movable in an atomic manner. This resolves a race condition issue where another entity could operate on memory blocks while the device is being removed, causing hot-unplug failures or deadlocks. The patch also updates the unbind process to prevent resource leaks and deadlock situations.

reviews.author: Richard Cheng
reviews.summary: Reviewer Richard Cheng noted that the state of a DAX kmem device is not updated when blocks are offlined through the old per-block interface, leading to inconsistent behavior and potential resource leaks. He suggested dropping an early return in the unbind path and attempting dax_kmem_remove_ranges() instead.
reviews.raw_body:
Hi Gregory,

I suppose data->state is only updated when writing to the new daxX.Y/state file?
If the blocks are offlined through the old per-block interface, data->state
still says online and we will hit this early return.
Everything is leaked and a later rebind fails -EBUSY.

Current behavior is using remove_memory(), and it succeeds on offlined blocks
and fails safely with -EBUSY on online ones, no offlining from unbind context
either way.

Your changelog state "on unbind fallback to legacy if still online", but the
fallback only runs for data->state == MMOP_OFFLINE.
Maybe drop this early return and just try dax_kmem_remove_ranges() here too?

Best regards,
Richard Cheng.

reviews.author: Gregory Price (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged that there is a race condition between dax/state and block/state when the whole device is offline, agreed to fix it in a new version.
reviews.raw_body:
Hm, I think you are right.  When doing some testing I bounced between
tests which race with dax/state and block/state, and I missed the
obvious case of the whole device being offline but dax/state being stale.

I think this entire chunk actually just drops to:

dax_kmem_remove_ranges(dev_dax, data);

And the internal error catches the condition to the log.

Thanks! I will spin a new version next week.

~Gregory

reviews.author: Dave Jiang
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that the kernel version referenced in the patch is outdated and requested an update to reflect the current version.
reviews.raw_body:
Kernel version a bit old :)

DJ

reviews.author: Gregory Price (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged that the swapoff path needs to drop the per-vswap spinlock before calling try_to_unmap(), agreed to restructure in v2, but did not provide a clear plan for addressing this issue.
reviews.raw_body:
It has been a hot second since i started this n_n;;;;

~Gregory

reviews.author: Dan (nvidia)
reviews.summary:
Reviewer Dan noted that the code previously called remove_memory() during unbind, which would fire a BUG() if any of the memory blocks were online at that time, and requested it be changed to a WARN() instead.

Reviewer noted that the memory_block device should grow a sysfs link back to the dev_dax kobject to indicate an alternate management interface for atomic removal

Reviewer Dan removed the Date and KernelVersion fields from the sysfs-bus-dax documentation as they are not informative, especially for kernels that never existed.

Reviewer Dan pointed out that the ranges array allocation in dax_kmem_do_hotremove() uses kmalloc_objs(), which may not be sufficient for large devices and requested a more robust memory allocation method.

Reviewer Dan pointed out that the current implementation of atomic whole-device hotplug using a sysfs interface does not handle probe failures correctly, as the dev_group attribute will still be present even if the device is unplugged.

Reviewer Dan (nvidia) questioned whether it's safe to start dax_kmem in unplugged mode, suggesting that drivers might want to preclude legacy per-block hotplug policy firing by starting in this state.

Reviewer Dan suggested replacing the dynamically created 'state' attribute with a statically declared one, citing that attributes appearing only while the driver is attached should be declared statically.

Reviewer Dan suggested keeping dev_err() for severe errors instead of lifting it to a WARN and removing hotremove if ->state is not DAX_KMEM_UNPLUGGED or MMOP_OFFLINE.

Reviewer Dan noted that if the 'state' sysfs attribute is registered statically, there will be one less cleanup step required.
reviews.raw_body:
Yeah, it looks like BUG behaviour was there from day 1 when it should
always have been a WARN.

---

A collection of fixups below. The broad strokes look good to me. The
only architecture feedback I have is whether a memory_block device
should grow a sysfs link back to the dev_dax kobject. If only to
indicate "hey, I have an alternate management interface over here for
atomic removal". That can be kicked down the road to a later patch.

With the fixups:

Reviewed-by: Dan Williams <djbw@kernel.org>

---

Date and KernelVersion do not really provide much info, especially when
documenting kernels that never existed. I have dropped them from my ABI
documentation.

---

The new hotness is:

ranges = kmalloc_objs(*ranges, dev_dax->nr_range);

---

Cannot happen with a dev_group attribute. If probe succeeds then drvdata
is present. If probe fails, attribute never appears.

When unplugging, dev_groups are unregistered before drvdata is cleared.

---

Unless maybe a driver knows better and wants to preclude the possibility
of legacy per-block hotplug policy firing? I.e. driver asks for dax_kmem
to start in the unplugged mode per my "should DAX_KMEM_UNPLUGGED be a
online_type with a different sentinel".

---

Always prefer statically declared attributes. In this case an attribute
that only appears while the driver is attached would be something like:

diff --git a/drivers/dax/kmem.c b/drivers/dax/kmem.c
index 2cc8749bc871..9be582a33be4 100644
--- a/drivers/dax/kmem.c
+++ b/drivers/dax/kmem.c
@@ -270,10 +270,22 @@ static void dev_dax_kmem_remove(struct dev_dax *dev_dax)
 }
 #endif /* CONFIG_MEMORY_HOTREMOVE */
 
+static DEVICE_ATTR_RW(state);
+
+static struct attribute *dev_dax_attrs[] = {
+        &dev_attr_state.attr,
+        NULL
+};
+
+ATTRIBUTE_GROUPS(dev_dax);
+
 static struct dax_device_driver device_dax_kmem_driver = {
        .probe = dev_dax_kmem_probe,
        .remove = dev_dax_kmem_remove,
        .type = DAXDRV_KMEM_TYPE,
+       .drv = {
+               .dev_groups = &dev_dax_groups,
+       },
 };
 
 static int __init dax_kmem_init(void)

---

I like that the BUG() is avoided, but I think these should stay
dev_err() given the severity.

---

One less cleanup to do if the attribute is registered statically.
Attributes are shutdown prior to this point.

reviews.author: Gregory Price (author)
reviews.summary:
Author acknowledged that mhp_online_type_to_str could potentially return NULL, and suggested adding a check to prevent a latent NULL dereference.

Author acknowledged a concern about differentiating between default and unplugged states in the sysfs attribute, agreed to add DAX_KMEM_UNPLUGGED to the header and allow differentiation.

Author acknowledged that they had to revert to calling remove_memory() by default due to feedback, but plans to modify the BUG() to a WARN() instead of making further changes.

Author acknowledges that the patch needs to handle the case where a device is online at unbind, and agrees to prevent deadlock by not attempting hotremove if ->state is not DAX_KMEM_UNPLUGGED or MMOP_OFFLINE.
reviews.raw_body:
mhp_online_type_to_str can technically return NULL, seems better to not
just let a NULL dereference sit latent even if we can visually tell it
can't happen today?

---

I suppose I can put DAX_KMEM_UNPLUGGED in the header and allow this.
That seems reasonable, and makes sense why to differentiate
DEFAULT/UNPLUGGED.

ack.

---

I had to go back to calling remove_memory() by default given different
feedback, but I think if anything I will just modify the BUG() to a
WARN() and call it a day.

ack on dev_err().

---

ack.

~Gregory

reviews.author: Dan (nvidia)
reviews.summary: Reviewer Dan noted that 'unknown' state in dax_kmem_get_state() is due to missing drvdata, and suggested this as an explanation for the behavior.
reviews.raw_body:
Oh, makes sense I was thinking "unknown" was only a result of the
drvdata missing case.

reviews.author: Gregory Price (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged that the original patch did not trigger a BUG() in the first place and is willing to update comments if reviewer wants to soften BUG() to WARN(), but will close this issue separately.
reviews.raw_body:
Ah, you know, on second look - DAX could never reach this BUG() in the
first place.  I think this was a hold-over from when i was originally
refactoring and trying to figure out the right path.

If there's a desire to soften these BUG() to WARN(), i can submit that
separately, but i will close this all out with some comment updates.

2026-07-09.patch_summary: This patch adds a sysfs interface to enable atomic whole-device hotplug operations for DAX kmem devices. The new 'state' attribute allows devices to be transitioned between states such as unplugged, online, and offline_kernel or offline_movable in an atomic manner. This resolves a race condition issue where another entity could operate on memory blocks while the device is being removed, causing hot-unplug failures or deadlocks. The patch also updates the unbind process to prevent resource leaks and deadlock situations.
